body:
### Contact Details

harshita.nagpal005@gmail.com

### Bug Description

The AI ticket analysis page reads the backend streaming response in `Frontend/src/user/pages/AIProcessing.jsx`, but it parses each network
  chunk independently.

  Currently the code does:

  ```js
  const chunk = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
  const lines = chunk.split('\n');

  Then it tries to parse every data: ... line immediately.

  This can break because streaming/SSE responses are not guaranteed to arrive as complete lines. A JSON payload can be split across two
  chunks, especially the final done event that contains the full ticket result. When that happens, JSON.parse fails and finalTicket may stay
  null, causing the app to fall back even though the backend returned a valid response.

  Files involved

  - Frontend/src/user/pages/AIProcessing.jsx
  - Backend stream source for reference: backend/main.py, /ai/analyze_stream



  Example approach:

  - Append each decoded chunk to buffer.
  - Split on \n\n because SSE events are separated by blank lines.
  - Keep the last incomplete event in buffer.
  - Only parse complete events that start with data:.

### Steps to Reproduce

  To Reproduce

  1. Go to the user flow for creating a ticket.
  2. Submit a ticket so the app navigates to /ai-processing.
  3. The frontend calls /ai/analyze_stream.
  4. If the browser receives a partial SSE line, the frontend may log a JSON parse error and fail to use the final AI result.

### Expected Behavior


  Expected behavior
  The frontend should buffer incomplete streamed text until a full SSE event is received, then parse the complete data: payload.

  Suggested fix
  Update the stream-reading logic in AIProcessing.jsx to keep a buffer string between chunks.


### Helpdesk.AI Version

main branch

comments.body: Hi, IÔÇÖm Dhevika, a GSSoC 2026 contributor in Open Source + AI/Agents Track. IÔÇÖd like to work on this issue. Could you please assign it to me?
Hi, I am SHRAWANI, a GSSoC 2026 contributor and would like to work on this. Could you please assign it to me ??
Hi @DHEVIKA, welcome to the project. I have assigned this issue to you. @Shrawani08 sorry, this issue has been assigned to the first person who claimed it. Please check out other open issues!

Hi @harshitanagpal05, I wanted to personally thank you for writing such an incredibly detailed bug report! Providing the exact root cause and suggested fix makes this a perfect learning opportunity for our GSSoC contributors. Great work!

Hi @DHEVIKA, thanks for stepping up to take this on! Handling SSE chunk buffering is a great frontend challenge. Let us know if you need any pointers while working on it. We're looking forward to your PR!

Hi @Shrawani08, thank you for your interest! While @DHEVIKA is currently taking the lead on this, we'd love for you to check out our other open issues. However, if you have any thoughts on how to structure the text buffer, feel free to share them here!
@ritesh-1918 Thank you  for assigning this issue for me

Hey @harshitanagpal05, @DHEVIKA, and @Shrawani08!

Before you dive into the code, you'll need to set up and access the application to test your changes. Here is the step-by-step guide to get access to the dashboard:

### ??? Contributor Onboarding Guide
1. **Create an Account:** Go to the sign-up page on your local build (usually http://localhost:5173/signup) or our deployed link.
2. **Select the Company:** During sign-up, you MUST select **Ritesh Private Limited** as the company.
3. **Verify Your Email:** Complete the email verification step first.
4. **Request Access:** After your email is confirmed, you need manual approval to access the user dashboard. You can either:
   - Send me a formal WhatsApp message requesting access.
   - Or, simply reply here on GitHub with the email address you used to sign up.
5. **Get Approved:** Once I receive your request, I will approve your account from the backend. You will then have full access to the user perspective to reproduce bugs and test your features!

Happy coding! Let me know when you've signed up.

Hi @ritesh-1918 , IÔÇÖve set up the project locally and reached the signup page, but **ÔÇ£Ritesh Private LimitedÔÇØ is not appearing as a selectable company** (it shows ÔÇ£No companies foundÔÇØ). Could you please check if thereÔÇÖs any additional setup needed (backend/env configuration) or help enable the company entry so I can complete signup and test the assigned issue? Thank you!

Hi @harshitanagpal05,

Thank you for reporting this issue and sharing such a detailed analysis of the SSE streaming/chunk parsing logic!

Regarding the behavior you observed with the project and user details:
Because you are running the project locally, the application is not dynamically switching or loading the correct project details and user information from the production database/live environment. This is why it is not showing the real user info or changing context as expected.

### Recommended Next Steps:
1. **Test on Deployment:** Please test the entire application flow using the **live, deployed link**(https://helpdeskaiv1.vercel.app/signup) rather than running it locally. This will ensure all live database interactions, user contexts, and production streaming integrations function correctly.
2. **Local Debugging & Improvements:** Once you have tested the live flow, if you find any definitive bugs or areas of improvement (such as the chunk buffering optimization for the SSE parsing in `AIProcessing.jsx` that you suggested), please feel free to replicate and work on fixing them locally, then submit a Pull Request!

Thank you again for your valuable contributions to Helpdesk.AI! Let us know if you have any questions.

Hi @ritesh-1918, This is Dhevika here... IÔÇÖve signed up successfully using **[dhev0611@gmail.com]** under **Ritesh Private Limited** and completed email verification. Kindly approve my account so I can access the dashboard and test the assigned issue. Thank you!

Also, as a next step once you complete the dashboard work: please try to go through the company registration page via the signup URL(https://helpdeskaiv1.vercel.app/admin-signup) yourself, and then try to access the app using the admin portal to see how that flow works. If you aren't comfortable creating the company registration yourself, let me know and I can provide you with the Ritesh Private Limited company details directly (but I will share those only through DMs or via personal mail for security).

Thanks for your contribution and happy testing!

title: [BUG] Fix AI streaming response parsing when SSE chunks are split
